briefdescription.para: Flag indicating that DPPI system consists of multiple domains (instances).
Flag indicating that there are fixed connections between PPIB and DPPI channels.
Handle used for PPI connection.
Handle used for PPI group resource.
Function for initializing GPPI with a structure with resources.

detaileddescription.para: Function shall be called as early as possible to allow resource allocation. p_instance contains channel resources which need to be initialized before calling that function.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting the domain ID from the group handle.
Function for getting the domain ID from the peripheral register address.
Function for getting the channel that is used for an endpoint.
Function for getting one of a channels that is used in a connection.

detaileddescription.para: A connection may consists of a number of nodes which are domains with a unique DPPI or PPIB nodes. nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get can be used to get the domain ID for a given peripheral. Node ID for PPIB is target-specific and can be found in a target-specific header file (see nrfx_gppi_node_id_t).

briefdescription.para: Function for allocating and setup a connection between two domains.

detaileddescription.para: In case of a multi domain DPPI, function is allocating all needed resources and configures PPIB bridges. For single domain cases (PPI or DPPI) only a channel is allocated.

parameterdescription.para: Domain that will produce (publish) events. Not used in single domain case.
Domain that will consume (subsribe to) events. Not used in single domain case.
Handle used to control the connection.
There is not enough resources to allocate the connection.

briefdescription.para: Function for attaching an endpoint to the connection.

detaileddescription.para: Endpoint can be task, event, publish or subscribe register address. In order to allow attaching the endpoint it must belong to the route that connection is using. On SoC with PPI there are limitations regarding how many endpoints can be attached to a handle (channel) - 1 event and 2 tasks (1 if FORK is not present).

parameterdescription.para: Endpoint cannot be attached, i.e. it does not belong to the route.

briefdescription.para: Function for attaching an endpoint to the channel.

detaileddescription.para: Endpoint can be task, event, publish or subscribe register address.

parameterdescription.para: Endpoint cannot be attached, i.e. endpoints already configured in case of PPI.

briefdescription.para: Function for allocating a connection between Task and Event.

detaileddescription.para: Function performs nrfx_gppi_domain_conn_alloc followed by the configuration of endpoints.

parameterdescription.para: Handle used to control the connection.
There is not enough resources to allocate the connection.
eep or tep is already configured to be used by the DPPI.

briefdescription.para: Function for freeing a connection between domains.

detaileddescription.para: For connection within a single domain a channel is freed. For cross-domain connection channels in bridges (PPIB) are cleared and all used resources are freed.. Connection shall be disabled prior to clearing.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing an endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: Remove endpoint from DPPI channel. For PPI it searches all channel configurations and clears the first configuration where the endpoint was used. For PPI it is more time consuming so in PPI specific code nrfx_gppi_ep_ch_clear can be used.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing an endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: For DPPI it is the same as nrfx_gppi_ep_clear. For PPI it clears channel configuration.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling an endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: In case of PPI function enables the channel which is used by the endpoint. In case of DPPI, function is only enabling an endpoint and does not control the channel to which the endpoint is attached.

briefdescription.para: Function for disabling an endpoint.

detaileddescription.para: In case of PPI function disables the channel which is used by the endpoint. In case of DPPI, function is only disabling the endpoint and does not control the channel to which the endpoint is attached.

briefdescription.para: Function for clearing and freeing a connection.

detaileddescription.para: Function performs nrfx_gppi_domain_conn_free followed by clearing of endpoints.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling a connection.

detaileddescription.para: Function enables channels in every DPPI instance which belongs to the connection.

briefdescription.para: Function for disabling a connection.

detaileddescription.para: Function disables channels in every DPPI instance which belongs to the connection.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling channels in a domain.

parameterdescription.para: Domain ID. nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get can be used to get domain_id.

briefdescription.para: Function for disabling channels in a domain.

parameterdescription.para: Domain ID. nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get can be used to get domain_id.

briefdescription.para: Function for checking if a channel is enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Domain ID. nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get can be used to get domain_id.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling a channel used by the endpoint.
Function for checking if a channel assigned to the endpoint is enabled.
Function for disabling a channel used by the endpoint.
Function for allocating a group for given set of endpoints.

detaileddescription.para: Group can only enable or disable channels in a single DPPI so all endpoints must belong to a single domain. Use nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get with one of the endpoints that is intended to be added to that group to get domain_id.

parameterdescription.para: Successful allocation of a group. handle can be used.
Endpoints are not from the same domain.
Failed to allocate a free group channel.

briefdescription.para: Function for freeing a group.
Function for adding a channel to a group.

detaileddescription.para: nrfx_gppi_ep_channel_get can be used on a configured endpoint to retrieve the channel that shall be added to the group.

briefdescription.para: Function for removing a channel from a group.

detaileddescription.para: nrfx_gppi_ep_channel_get can be used on a configured endpoint to retrieve the channel that shall be added to the group.

briefdescription.para: Function for adding a configured endpoint to a group.

detaileddescription.para: Endpoint must be from the same domain as group and it must already be configured to use a channel.

briefdescription.para: Function for removing a configured endpoint from a group.

detaileddescription.para: Endpoint must be added to the group and it must be configured to use a channel.

parameterdescription.para: Failed to remove from the group.

briefdescription.para: Function for getting a mask with channels which are assigned to the group.

simplesect.para: Mask with bits set for channels that belong to a group.

briefdescription.para: Function for enabling a group.
Function for disabling a group.
Function for getting the enable group task address.
Function for getting the disable group task address.
Function for allocating a group channel in a specific node of the DPPI system.

detaileddescription.para: Function can be used to allocate a resource and use it outside of this driver.

simplesect.para: node_id parameter is used only in the system with multiple domains. nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get or target-specific header can be used to get the node ID (see nrfx_gppi_node_id_t).

parameterdescription.para: Target-specific identifier of the DPPI system node (DPPIC or PPIB).

briefdescription.para: Function for freeing a channel in a specific node of the DPPI system.

detaileddescription.para: channel must be allocated earlier using nrfx_gppi_channel_alloc. node_id must be the same as the one used in nrfx_gppi_channel_alloc for allocating that resource.

parameterdescription.para: Target-specific identifier of the DPPI system node (DPPIC or PPIB).

briefdescription.para: Function for allocating a group channel in a specific node of the DPPI system.

detaileddescription.para: Function can be used to allocate a resource and use it outside of this driver.

simplesect.para: domain_id parameter is used only in the system with multiple domains. Use nrfx_gppi_domain_id_get to get the ID.

parameterdescription.para: Target-specific identifier of the DPPI system node.

briefdescription.para: Function for freeing a group channel in a specific node of the DPPI system.

detaileddescription.para: channel must be allocated earlier using nrfx_gppi_group_channel_alloc. domain_id must be the same as the one used in nrfx_gppi_group_channel_alloc for allocating that resource.

parameterdescription.para: Target-specific identifier of the DPPI system node.

briefdescription.para: Function for allocating and setting up a connection using an external resource.

detaileddescription.para: If DPPI resource is provided then it is used in the connection and additional channel in that domain is not allocated. If connection was set up with an external resource then functions for enabling and disabling the connection are only changing state of channels that were allocated for that connection. If p is null then function is equivalent of nrfx_gppi_domain_conn_alloc.

parameterdescription.para: Domain that will consume (subsribe to) events.
Handle used to control the connection.
There is not enough resources to allocate the connection.
Not supported. Supported only on multi domain system.
